title: Chocolate frangipane tart
subtitle: Chocolate pastry with almond frangipane and raspberry jam
excerpt: A dark chocolate shortcrust shell spread with raspberry jam and filled with a soft almond frangipane. Baked until the frangipane is set and the pastry is crisp. The jam layer provides sharpness between the two dense elements. Scatter flaked almonds over the top before baking.
sourceNotes: The frangipane tart in the French tradition (tarte Bourdaloue, Bakewell tart) pairs almond cream with a pastry shell and fruit preserve. This recipe synthesises the classic format with a chocolate shortcrust and the addition of raspberry jam as an acidic middle layer. The approach draws on the established British and French almond tart tradition.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool completely and freeze the whole tart. Thaw at room temperature for 2 hours.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The tart shell can be blind-baked a day ahead. The assembled tart keeps well at room temperature for 2 days, refrigerated for up to 4 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the frangipane filling bakes in 35 to 40 minutes until set and golden on top

glossaryTerms.definition: Frangipane is done when the top is golden and a skewer inserted into the centre comes out clean, with no wet batter. The top should feel firm and springy when pressed gently. Under-baked frangipane is dense and gluey in the centre; over-baked becomes dry. Check at 35 minutes and give it 5 more minutes if needed.

content.text: This tart is built in layers: chocolate pastry, raspberry jam, almond frangipane. The contrast between the three elements is the point. Checking
by skewer is more reliable than timing alone, as ovens vary significantly.
Make and chill the pastry shell

Sift together 180 g flour and 20 g cocoa. Rub in 100 g cold butter. Add 40 g icing sugar, 1 egg yolk, and 1 to 2 tbsp cold water. Mix to a firm dough. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Heat the oven to 190 degrees C fan. Roll to 3 mm and line a greased 23 cm loose-bottomed tart tin. Chill for 15 minutes. Line with baking paper and baking weights. Blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove paper and weights and bake for 5 to 8 minutes more until dry. Reduce oven to 180 degrees C fan.

Spread 100 g raspberry jam over the base of the warm pastry shell. Beat 150 g softened butter with 150 g caster sugar until pale. Beat in 3 eggs one at a time. Stir in 150 g almond flour and 1 tsp vanilla. Spoon the frangipane over the jam and spread level. Scatter 30 g flaked almonds over the top. Bake at 180 degrees C fan for 35 to 40 minutes until the frangipane is set and golden and a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tin before releasing.

items.problem: Jam bubbles up through the frangipane during baking
items.solution: The jam layer was too thick. Use no more than 100 g for a 23 cm tin. A thin, even layer prevents it from boiling up through the frangipane as it expands in the heat.

items.problem: Frangipane is set at the edges but still wet in the centre
items.solution: The tart needs longer. Give it another 5 minutes. If the top is browning too fast, tent loosely with foil for the final 10 minutes.
